title: Financial Analytics Market Worth $36.10 Billion by 2035 as AI and Predictive Analytics Transform Enterprise Finance | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global financial analytics market is projected to grow from $12.15 billion in 2025 to $36.10 billion by 2035, driven by AI adoption, cloud computing, and predictive analytics. North America leads with 35% market share, while Europe and Asia Pacific show strong growth potential. Large enterprises dominate, but SMEs are expected to grow fastest due to affordable cloud-based solutions.

title: Payment Gateway Market Opportunity Report 2026: Drivers, Trends, and Forecasts 2025-2035 - Growing Blockchain and Cryptocurrency Integration and Emerging Markets with Underbanked Populations
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global payment gateway market is projected to grow from USD 32.7 billion in 2025 to USD 116.7 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 14.1%. Growth is driven by rapid digital payment adoption, e-commerce expansion, mobile penetration, and increasing demand for secure transactions. Hosted payment gateways lead with 52.3% market share, while card-based payments capture 47%. Key opportunities include blockchain integration and emerging markets with underbanked populations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key global player in the payment gateway market with strong competitive positioning. Benefits from the projected 14.1% CAGR market growth and increasing digital payment adoption worldwide.
Key player in the dominant cards segment (47% market share, USD 15.4 billion). Benefits from global card network infrastructure and established consumer confidence in card-based transactions.
Major player in the cards segment with global acceptance and established infrastructure. Positioned to benefit from continued dominance of card-based payments across online and offline channels.

title: The Quiet Infrastructure Play on Small-Bank Survival
author: Peter Frank
description: Jack Henry & Associates, a fintech infrastructure provider serving 7,400 community banks and credit unions, demonstrates strong financial performance with 7.9% revenue growth and 27.4% net income growth in Q2 FY2026. The company benefits from high recurring revenue (92% of sales) and expanding margins, though faces headwinds from bank consolidation and competition from larger rivals Fiserv and Fidelity National Information Services. Analysts rate it a Moderate Buy with a $200 price target.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong earnings beat (EPS $1.72 vs. $1.43 expected), significant margin expansion (25.7% vs. 21.4% YoY), nearly doubled free cash flow, raised full-year guidance, and analyst consensus of Moderate Buy with meaningful upside to $200 target. Dominant market position with high switching costs and recurring revenue model provides stability.

Mentioned as a larger competitor several times larger than Jack Henry, actively pursuing smaller banks with expanded cloud platforms. Represents competitive pressure but no specific performance data provided in the article.

Identified as another major competitor several times larger than Jack Henry, expanding cloud platforms and pursuing smaller banks. Represents competitive threat but no specific performance metrics discussed.

title: 3 Different Fintech Giants: Turnaround, Stability, or Risky Bet?
author: Peter Frank
description: The article analyzes three major fintech payment processors with distinct profiles: Fiserv faces a turnaround challenge with stock near 8-year lows despite strong cash flow; Global Payments pursues a complex transformation with its $24 billion Worldpay acquisition; and FIS offers stability with predictable growth and an attractive 4% dividend yield. Each company represents different risk-reward profiles within the booming payments industry.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock trades near 8-year lows despite strong fundamentals; disappointing Q3 results led to management changes; 2026 guidance below expectations with only 1-3% organic revenue growth; analyst consensus is Hold with modest price targets in low-to-mid $70s, suggesting limited near-term upside.

Complex story with mixed signals; $24 billion Worldpay acquisition offers growth potential but carries integration risks; adjusted earnings up 11% but GAAP results declined; analyst sentiment is lukewarm with Hold consensus and modest price targets in upper $80s; stock recently hit 52-week low despite optimistic 2026 projections.

Most balanced profile with predictable 5% revenue growth and 10% EPS growth; strong cash flow generation with 19% increase; attractive 4% dividend yield with recent 10% increase; Wall Street consensus is Moderate Buy with average price target of $69.67 representing ~50% upside; positioned as most stable income play in the sector.

title: 2 Top Dividend Stocks to Buy in February
author: Dave Kovaleski
description: The article recommends two dividend stocks for February: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS), a fintech company that recently divested its underperforming Worldpay acquisition and acquired Issuer Solutions, with analysts projecting 67% upside and a recent 10% dividend increase; and Main Street Capital (MAIN), a business development company paying monthly dividends at a 7.20% yield with 18 consecutive years of dividend increases and a five-year annualized return of 11%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 63% over five years due to poor Worldpay acquisition, but recent divestment and acquisition of Issuer Solutions business is seen as a better strategic fit. Analysts rate it a consensus buy with 67% upside potential, and the company just increased its dividend by 10% to $0.44 per share at 3.62% yield.

Highly attractive dividend stock paying monthly dividends at 7.20% yield, with 18 consecutive years of dividend increases. As a BDC required to distribute 90% of taxable income, it also pays supplemental dividends quarterly. Five-year annualized return of 11% demonstrates reliable performance.
Mentioned only as the company from which Fidelity acquired the Issuer Solutions business. No direct investment recommendation or analysis provided.
